arts.ttl: Plymouth scrap documentary about relegation fight after Rooney’s exit
arts.sum: Project driven by Wayne Rooney and his advisers Production company made Coleen Rooney documentary Plymouth have scrapped plans to make a documentary about their battle against relegation from the Championship after Wayne Rooney’s departure this week. Filming was paused last month as a result of the downturn in results that eventually led to Rooney’s exit and there are no plans to resume. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Mark Robins's two-word response to Stoke City being dubbed 'an impossible job'
arts.sum: If you’re working with good people and you’ve got the same interests and beliefs and a way of doing things then that’s something we already have in common. I do know what I’m good at and I do know what I need help with and I’ve brought people in to help with that, I’ve got people already here to help with other things.

arts.ttl: 'Hungry' Robins plans top-flight return for Stoke
arts.sum: New Stoke City boss Mark Robins is aiming to lift the Potters back into the Premier League after a six-and-a-half year absence.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins ready to 'get teeth into Stoke City and put it right' as he holds first press conference
arts.sum: But he spoke about how the club is still on a small list of jobs he would want – and how he will work with sporting director Jon Walters and chairman John Coates to make sure everything is in place to make sure there is a sustained change of direction. If we can get to that point then things will move forward quicker and it's transferable through football clubs but it is a skill and you can't just jump in because you can easily make a mess of things but I think with both of our experiences, we'll be absolutely fine and it won't be through want of trying.”

arts.ttl: Coleen Rooney's heartbreaking response after husband Wayne sacked by Plymouth
arts.sum: Plymouth Argyle and Wayne Rooney parted ways after just a few months, with the Devon club bottom of the Championship after failing to win away from home all season

arts.ttl: Stoke City live as Mark Robins holds first press conference
arts.sum: “I watched the game against Sunderland the other day (a 1-0 victory for the Potters), and that epitomised what I think Stoke City supporters want. Mark Robins, in his first interview since being appointed, he says: “This is a really good club.

arts.ttl: Football Daily | Was Wayne Rooney drinking in management’s Last Chance Saloon?
arts.sum: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Sacked by Birmingham City on 2 January last year after steering the club from sixth place to 20th in the Championship inside 15 games, then mutually relieved of his duties by Plymouth Argyle with the club rock-bottom of the table 363 days later, it has not gone unnoticed that 2024 was something of a managerial annus horribilis for Wayne Rooney. He suffered the ignominy of being both the first and last of the – we make it – 21 different managers from England’s second tier to take an unceremonious step through assorted doors marked “Do One!” in the calendar year that’s just drawn to a close. Happy new year! A hearty rendition of ‘Hey Jude’ rang out at Brentford yesterday, but given that Arsenal’s No 9 has rediscovered his scoring form, I reckon that Jesus is more popular than the Beatles (in north London, anyway)” – Peter Oh. I don’t know who to feel more sympathy for: Julen Lopetegui or Ruben Amorim. After much consideration, it has to be Amorim, who has gone from having a fast young energetic successful squad at Sporting to managing a relegation-threatened team with one superstar on massive wages barely able to make the squad, and what looked like a 47-year-old struggling badly to perform at centre-half in a poor Sunday League team” – John Weldon. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Mark Robins' Stoke City arrival means new dynamic set to play out
arts.sum: By all accounts, Stoke City have made an excellent appointment in naming Mark Robins as their latest first-team boss. The former Coventry City man takes over from Narcis Pelach, who like Steven Schumacher before him, was Stoke’s head coach, not manager.

arts.ttl: 'Very sad' Wayne Rooney gets new job as he returns home to Coleen
arts.sum: Coleen Rooney's career is going from strength to strength after her appearance on I'm a Celebrity...Get Me Out of Here! - while husband Wayne is out of work

arts.ttl: Exit confirmed as Stoke City finalise Mark Robins' back room team
arts.sum: Mark Robins's first team coaching set-up at Stoke City has been confirmed with two arrivals, two staying, one shifting and the exit of Dean Whitehead. Rowberry's CV includes eight years working at Cardiff, from youth to first team level, and a spell as head coach of hometown team Newport County.
